name: The Precondition Fulfilled
description: The SCAMP evidence is out. Ironheart is with the person who said yes first. The Architect said the precondition is someone who understands what the choice is. The Grid node is waiting.
accept_text: Vespa is at the market, exactly where she always is. The case is beside her on the stall — she hasn't put it away. When you tell her Vane moved, that the transmission went through, she is still for a moment with the particular quality of someone checking an internal list and finding it complete. 'The Architect told me to wait until the shape was ready,' she says. 'I've been waiting for eight months. I think the shape is ready.' She picks up the case. She looks toward the Spire, the direction she always looks, and this time it seems like she might be listening rather than looking. 'The node is below the market. I know where the junction is — I've been in the tunnel maintenance network for forty years.' She stands. 'You should come. The Architect said the precondition is someone who understands what the choice is.' She looks at you steadily. 'That's you. I am the person who says yes. You are the person who understood what yes means.' She picks up her tin, closes it, sets it back on the stall. Leaves it there. 'Let's go.'
complete_text: The junction beneath the Silent Market is older than the market, older than the war. The crystal here is not battlefield Aetherium. It is something else — the original lattice, from before the Cascade, still intact at this depth, warm in a way that has nothing to do with the Grid restart eight months ago. This warmth has been here for nine hundred years. Vespa knows the room. She goes to the node without hesitation — a crystal formation at the center, roughly chest height, humming at a frequency you feel in the back of your teeth. Arlen is here, or the part of Arlen that lives in the lattice is here. You can feel it as attention, as presence. Not alone in the room even before the warmth of the Architect arrives — the specific warmth, the one you know from the Spire, the one that has the quality of being understood. 'You came,' the Architect says. It sounds the way it sounded in the Spire. Like the room is speaking. 'Both of you.' It is quiet for a moment. 'The evidence is out. The lattice registered the transmission — both simultaneously, at two minutes past midnight. The Silent moved against the filing seventeen minutes later. Seventeen minutes was enough.' The warmth settles. 'The third thing that was needed was this. Someone who understands what the second completion is, standing in the room where the precondition can be registered.' Vespa sets the case on the floor and opens it. She lifts Ironheart. The hammer is warm — it has always been warm, it has been warm for eleven hundred years, it has been warm since Kaelen Ironhand stood at the siege gate for six hours and chose not to swing. She holds it above the node crystal. She looks at you. 'I said yes eight months ago,' she says. 'I said yes because the Architect told me the time was when someone who understood the question said yes. I understood the question.' She is quiet. 'But I want to make sure. I want to ask again, now, with you here who understood what I couldn't explain to anyone else.' She holds the hammer over the crystal and she looks at you. Just looks. The Architect does not speak. Arlen does not speak. The lattice hums at that frequency, the one you feel in your teeth. You are not asked to swing. You are not asked to decide. You are asked only to be present — to be the person in the room who understands. You are present. Vespa sets the hammer on the node. Not a blow. A placement, deliberate and gentle, the way you set something down when you've been carrying it a long time and you've found where it belongs. The warmth changes. Extends. Something registers in the lattice the way a key turns in a lock — not loudly, not dramatically. A fact becoming different from what it was a moment ago. The second completion is not tonight. It is forty years away, if the rate holds. But tonight, in the deep lattice, something is different: a yes exists in the record. From someone who understood the question. The Architect is quiet for a long time. Then: 'The precondition is met.' Another silence. 'Is there anything you want to tell me?' The question is open. You could tell it about Rund — the man in the sealed mines, still there, still keeping records, still saying still warm. You could say nothing. The moment does not demand action from you. It has never demanded action from you. What it demanded was presence. Whatever you choose to say or not say, the hammer rests on the node. The fact exists. The warmth is everywhere.
